incident.name: Garden Grove MMA Tank Leak
incident.status_headline: As of May 25 2026, 50,000 residents remain under mandatory evacuation orders; OCFA conducted an all-night mission after discovering a crack in the GKN Aerospace MMA tank that may be relieving internal pressure, with results still being assessed and no resolution confirmed.

evacuation.boundary_text: North of Trask Avenue, south of Ball Road, east of Valley View Street, west of Dale Street; includes portions of West Anaheim, Cypress, Buena Park, and the city of Stanton

official_statements.text: Interim Chief TJ McGovern: Firefighters gathered 'positive intel' overnight during a recon mission late Saturday; crews identified a potential crack in the tank that may be relieving some internal pressure. An all-night mission is being conducted to confirm if pressure has been released and the threat of catastrophic explosion eliminated.

Captain Brian Yau: 'So far, it's been positive news because there is no vapors being detected by the air monitors, and there is no active leak that is on the ground. So far, that is a good sign.' But added the tank still has the possibility of catastrophic failure.

Division Chief Craig Covey: 'There are literally two options left remaining â€” either the tank fails and spills thousands of gallons of chemicals, or the tank goes into a thermal runaway and blows up.' He also said if the tank explodes it could be the largest hazmat incident in California history.

The internal temperature of the unstable tank is now over 100 degrees Fahrenheit; the gauge does not read higher than 100, so the exact temperature is unknown. Temperature had been rising approximately 1 degree per hour as of Saturday morning.

Governor Gavin Newsom declared a state of emergency in Orange County in response to the incident and requested that President Trump issue a federal Emergency Declaration to activate FEMA resources and federal funding.
